facts.evidence: Commit e3bd216e message states 'Operator decisions recorded: saas/ deleted (not worth converging)'. phase14_2_saas_and_eos_decision.json dec-142-01 records explicit operator decision. phase14_1r_canonicality_decisions.json confirms operator_approved: true.
phase14_1_saas_inspection.json exists with full structural inspection: package.json, tsconfig, drizzle config, 14 tables, 12 routes, migration journal, schema drift analysis, overlap analysis.

facts.claim: /opt/OS/saas was classified as redundant partial EOS backend/shell
facts.evidence: Inspection summary: 'saas/ is a Hono-based TypeScript backend service named eos-projection... No frontend, no deployment config.' Classification: 'backend_service'. Canonicality report: 'saas/ was a redundant partial backend/shell'. dec-142-01 rationale: 'Severe schema drift, no frontend, divergent auth.'

facts.claim: transports/api/http/server.ts already contains UMH platform API entrypoint
facts.evidence: server.ts contains Hono app with serve(), /health endpoint, authMiddleware, routes for organism/governance/chat/knowledge/execution/settings/config/system, error handler, notFound handler. All 7 checks pass.

facts.claim: transports/api/http owns platform routes, auth middleware, DB client, schema, health check, error handlers
facts.evidence: ls -R transports/api/http/ shows: db/ (client.ts, migrate.ts, schema.ts), middleware/ (auth.ts, operator.ts), routes/ (8 route files), lib/ (python_bridge.ts), server.ts (health + error handlers), types.ts, drizzle.config.ts.

facts.claim: /opt/OS/saas added EOS projection routes on top
facts.evidence: git show e3bd216e --stat shows deletion of 12 route files: activity.ts, agent.ts, agents.ts, analytics.ts, approvals.ts, events.ts, interactions.ts, outcomes.ts, skills.ts, tasks.ts, ventures.ts, workflows.ts. Inspection confirmed 'umh_platform_routes_mounted_in_api_index' showing saas/ imported from transports/api/http/.

facts.claim: No UMH platform API depends on /opt/OS/saas
facts.evidence: grep -r 'saas/' across *.py, *.ts, *.js found 0 import/require statements ('NO ACTIVE IMPORTS FROM SAAS'). Remaining references are string literals in guard scripts and organism engine data strings.

facts.claim: Only references to saas/ remaining are historical docs or dependency checker guards
facts.evidence: Code references: 4 pre-commit guard scripts (check_dependency_direction.py, check_type_divergence.py, check_instance_leak.py, check_projection_leak.py) contain 'saas/' as exclusion paths. 3 organism files contain 'saas/' as string literals in readiness checks, world model topology, and reconciliation data. 22 markdown doc files reference saas/ historically. Zero are active imports.
grep for 'from saas', 'import saas', 'require.*saas' across all .py/.ts/.js files returned 'NO ACTIVE IMPORTS FROM SAAS'.

facts.claim: Deletion is recoverable from git history
facts.evidence: git log --oneline --all -- saas/ returns 10+ commits including e3bd216e and prior history. All 30 files recoverable via 'git show e3bd216e~1:saas/<file>'.
git show e3bd216e --stat shows exactly '30 files changed, 6074 deletions(-)'. Matches claim precisely.

transports/api/http/server.ts is self-contained — imports all 8 route modules from local ./routes/, has health endpoint, auth middleware, error handling. No dependency on saas/. Server loads independently.
phase14_1_test_gate_results.json: 79/79 tests passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. Safety checks all true (no_fake_data, no_secret_leakage, no_unsafe_deletion, no_external_write, no_destructive_sync).

facts.claim: No source truth was lost because EOS canonical source is Beast/GitHub app
facts.evidence: phase14_1_canonicality_candidate_report.json maps 4 EOS sources; Beast has 603 files (most complete). phase14_1r_canonicality_decisions.json records operator-approved decision: 'Beast/GitHub EntrepreneurOS app is canonical EOS source'. phase14_2_saas_and_eos_decision.json dec-142-02 confirms Beast is canonical.

residual_references.note: All residual references are non-functional: guard script exclusion paths, organism topology/reconciliation data strings, and historical documentation. None cause runtime behavior.
